name: Montes de Valdorba – variante circular a la Leozarra por Ekisoain y Olleta (BTT Zona Media)
region_intro: Mountain biking around Leoz offers a diverse landscape characterized by rolling hills, dense forests, and varied terrain suitable for off-road cycling. The region features numerous ascents and descents, providing a dynamic experience for mountain bikers. Trails often wind through natural settings, incorporating elements like root sections and rocky outcrops. This area provides a range of routes that cater to different skill levels, from flowing paths to more technical sections. Best mountain bike trails around Leoz The most popular mountain bike trail is Tafalla and Olite Royal Palace Loop, a 16.7 miles (26.9 km) trail that takes about 2 hours to complete. This route offers a moderate challenge with gentle elevation changes. Another top favourite among local mountain bikers is Montes de Valdorba – circular desde Pueyo por el cerro San Pelaio, a moderate 17.8 miles (28.7 km) path. This route features significant climbs through varied terrain, including forested sections. Local mountain bikers also love the Ujué por el Canal de Navarra – circular por el Txutxu Alto, a 17.1 miles (27.6 km) trail leading through a mix of open landscapes and elevated sections, often completed in about 2 hours 35 minutes. Mountain biking around Leoz is defined by rolling hills, dense woodlands, and varied natural trails. The network offers options for different ability levels, from moderate paths to more challenging routes with significant elevation gain. The routes in Leoz are highly rated by the komoot community with an average score of 4.4 stars from around 40 reviews. More than 500 mountain bikers have used komoot to explore Leoz's varied terrain.
region_faq:
Q: How many mountain bike trails are available around Leoz?
A: Leoz offers a wide selection of mountain bike trails, with over 130 routes to explore. These include 13 easy, 104 moderate, and 20 difficult trails, catering to various skill levels.

Q: What kind of terrain can I expect when mountain biking in Leoz?
A: The terrain around Leoz is characterized by rolling hills, dense forests, and varied natural surfaces. You'll encounter numerous ascents and descents, with trails often featuring root sections and rocky outcrops, providing a dynamic off-road cycling experience.

Q: Are there any family-friendly mountain bike trails in Leoz?
A: Yes, Leoz has 13 easy mountain bike trails that are suitable for families or those looking for a less strenuous ride. These routes typically have gentler elevation changes and less technical terrain.

Q: What do other mountain bikers enjoy most about the trails in Leoz?
A: The mountain bike trails in Leoz are highly rated by the komoot community, with an average score of 4.3 stars from nearly 40 reviews. Riders often praise the diverse landscape, the mix of flowing paths and technical sections, and the natural settings that the routes wind through.

Q: Are there any circular mountain bike routes in Leoz?
A: Yes, many of the trails in Leoz are circular. For example, the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e988413495/montes-de-valdorba-circular-desde-pueyo-por-el-cerro-san-pelaio" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Montes de Valdorba – circular desde Pueyo por el cerro San Pelaio&lt;/a&gt; is a popular moderate route featuring significant climbs through forested sections. Another option is the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e988460185/ujue-por-el-canal-de-navarra-circular-por-el-txutxu-alto" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Ujué por el Canal de Navarra – circular por el Txutxu Alto&lt;/a&gt;, which leads through open landscapes and elevated areas.

Q: What are some notable landmarks or points of interest I can see near the mountain bike trails?
A: Several interesting landmarks are accessible near the trails. You could visit the historic &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/2704481" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Royal Palace of Olite&lt;/a&gt;, a magnificent medieval castle. Other points of interest include the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/6250557" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Church of Santa María la Real&lt;/a&gt; and the ancient &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/5267236" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Medieval bridge of Salinas de Ibargoiti&lt;/a&gt;. For panoramic views, consider trails that pass near &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/5187583" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Itzaga Peak&lt;/a&gt;.

Q: Are there any longer, more challenging mountain bike routes for experienced riders?
A: Experienced riders will find 20 difficult trails in Leoz. A longer, moderate option that offers a significant challenge is the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e988477695/altos-de-valgorra-y-monte-plano-circular-desde-san-martin-de-unx-btt-zona-media" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Altos de Valgorra y Monte Plano – circular desde San Martín de Unx (BTT Zona Media)&lt;/a&gt;, which spans over 55 kilometers and includes substantial elevation changes.

Q: What is the typical duration for mountain bike trails in Leoz?
A: Trail durations vary depending on the route and your pace. For example, the popular &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e988407444/tafalla-and-olite-royal-palace-loop" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Tafalla and Olite Royal Palace Loop&lt;/a&gt; typically takes about 2 hours to complete, while the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e988460185/ujue-por-el-canal-de-navarra-circular-por-el-txutxu-alto" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Ujué por el Canal de Navarra – circular por el Txutxu Alto&lt;/a&gt; is closer to 2 hours and 35 minutes.

Q: Are there any routes with significant elevation gain for a good workout?
A: Yes, many routes in Leoz feature significant elevation gain. The &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e989975255/higa-de-monreal-loop" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Higa de Monreal Loop&lt;/a&gt;, for instance, includes over 670 meters of ascent, providing a demanding ride through varied landscapes.

Q: Where can I find parking for mountain biking trailheads in Leoz?
A: While specific parking details for each trailhead are best found on individual route pages, many trails around Leoz start from villages or accessible points where local parking is available. It's advisable to check the komoot tour details for the specific route you plan to ride, as they often include information on starting points and parking.
tips: The top of Ekisoain is located at 1,071 meters above sea level and offers beautiful views of Higa de Monreal and the Montes de Valdorba.

This small but very picturesque town in the heart of Monte de Valdorba will give you the feeling of traveling back in time. It is surrounded by a wonderful landscape and its stone houses, many abandoned, make a beautiful postcard to remember.
